name: urlpy

metadata.name: urlpy
metadata.summary: Simple URL parsing, canonicalization and equivalence.

documentation:
A library with helper functions to parse URLs, and sanitize and normalize them
in pure python.

This includes support for escaping, unescaping, cleaning and sorting parameters
and query strings, and a little more sanitization.

This version is a friendly fork of the upstream url.py from Moz to keep a pure
Python version around to run on Python 2 and 3 and all OSes.

modules.name: urlpy
documentation:
This is a module for dealing with urls. In particular, sanitizing them.
This version is a friendly fork of the upstream from Moz to keep a pure Python
version around to run on Python on all OSes.
It also uses an alternate publicsuffix list provider package.
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: urlpy.parse
documentation: Parse the provided url string and return an URL object

classes.name: urlpy.URL

methods.name: urlpy.URL.parse
documentation: Parse the provided url, and return a URL instance

methods.name: urlpy.URL.copy
documentation: Return a new instance of an identical URL.

methods.name: urlpy.URL.equiv
documentation: Return true if this url is equivalent to another

methods.name: urlpy.URL.__eq__
documentation: Return true if this url is /exactly/ equal to another

methods.name: urlpy.URL.canonical
documentation:
Canonicalize this url. This includes reordering parameters and args
to have a consistent ordering

methods.name: urlpy.URL.defrag
documentation: Remove the fragment from this url

methods.name: urlpy.URL.deparam
documentation: Strip any of the provided parameters out of the url

methods.name: urlpy.URL.filter_params
documentation: Remove parameters if function(name, value)

methods.name: urlpy.URL.deuserinfo
documentation: Remove any userinfo

methods.name: urlpy.URL.abspath
documentation: Clear out any '..' and excessive slashes from the path

methods.name: urlpy.URL.sanitize
documentation: A shortcut to abspath and escape

methods.name: urlpy.URL.remove_default_port
documentation: If a port is provided an is the default, remove it.

methods.name: urlpy.URL.escape
documentation: Make sure that the path is correctly escaped

methods.name: urlpy.URL.unescape
documentation: Unescape the path

methods.name: urlpy.URL.relative
documentation: Evaluate the new path relative to the current url

methods.name: urlpy.URL.punycode
documentation: Convert to punycode hostname

methods.name: urlpy.URL.unpunycode
documentation: Convert to an unpunycoded hostname

methods.name: urlpy.URL.hostname
documentation: Return the hostname of the url.

methods.name: urlpy.URL.pld
documentation:
Return the 'pay-level domain' of the url
(http://moz.com/blog/what-the-heck-should-we-call-domaincom)

methods.name: urlpy.URL.tld
documentation: Return the top-level domain of a url

methods.name: urlpy.URL.absolute
documentation:
Return True if this is a fully-qualified URL with a hostname and
everything

methods.name: urlpy.URL.unicode
documentation: Return a utf-8 version of this url

For more information on how and what we parse / sanitize:
    http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc1808.html
The more up-to-date RFC is this one:
    http://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc3986.txt
